reviews.review:
This game made me hate bridges and those that construct them.
3/10

If this is your thing, look for it on sale.

Overall I'd say....meh
The physics are sadly quite terrible, so this is still a fun game to play, but many constructions don't make sense physically all too much.
This game\'s physics are kind of stupid sometimes but I just can\'t be mad. Some of the garbage thats happened to me has been hilarious! I love it! 10/10 exploding tanker trucks!

Recent "expansion" forces you to buy DLC to complete new achievements.  Why do this to a four year old game?  How about making a new game instead of messing with our achievement percentages?  And that was after taking ages to remove a broken achievement.  Their priorities seem to be profit before fun and profit before quality.  Don't give them your money.

If you're after achievements, don't even bother with this game.  After roughly 3 years since its release on Steam, well beyond when anyone who owns the game would care to reinstall it, they release a DLC...with new achievements...and seasonal achievements...that, as of writing, will not be obtainable for several months from now.

It's a kick below the belt, but what else can you expect from a phone-to-pc app game?
I really, really despise in-game sales. Recent updates added these horrible malfeatures. I uninstalled. Plenty of other games to play.
Last time I checked, this is pretty bad
fuck

Fun for about 1 hour, then the budget restrictions and materials restrictions just make it tedious.
Zero replayability imho, glad I only paid a couple bucks for it at the humble store.

This game is hilariously fun. Fling trucks into rivers, get sick air, watch as your rickety crap-bridge somehow holds the weight of a car filled with people who clearly have way too much faith in my construction skills wihtout knowing I'm just trying to save cash so I can pocket the money and run. 11/10, would exaggerate this review score again.
This allows me to build bridges, nice game.
Great game

Get some friends over, get drunk, start trash talking each other's bridges.

A delightful game that is very easy to boot up for 20 minutes, but can easily soak a few hours on occaision.
Could use more music and different backgrounds, but it's still a nice game to burn some time.

I liked it pretty much. It´s more fun and than i thought at the Beginning. I recommend it to everyone who like Physikbased Games or just want to destroy things :D. I would say to buy it on sale, the full price is still pretty expensive for just 5 hrs of gameplay.
Way to hard for average person...very frustrating after you get into the game a while..
Nice little game.  I especially like the fact that there isn't only one solution to most of the challenges.

Giving negative review just for tying one achievement to f*ckbook. Also it doesn't support Steam cloud for saves. But it probably worth it if you like bridge games, like to have some achievements and very basic story and can get it for 1 euro from Humble Store. Not 9 euros worth at all.

UPD: removed Facebook achievement. Good. Very basic. But ok. Changing to positive review.

Amazingly fun little game picked it up cheap got 3 games for the price of one and it is really good game will play the other games as well fun and a challenge need more games like this it is a little gem
I tend to swear a lot at games, but no game has ever made me yelll 'Fuck yes!' as much as this game has.

Silly game, silly physics, puzzles were fun. 

7.5/10
GOOD GAME 10/10

Bought this a while ago on sale thinking it'd be an interesting to waste some time in. But it's not very fun, after a while the resource cap is the main issue. Also the solutions don't really feel like they can very that much so later on I ended up running trial and error over and over figuring out which wood beams needed to be replaced with metal ones. It really feels like a mobile game to me.
gud
Very entertaining game for short breaks.

Ever played Bridge It? Yes you old fart the one that came free with top end Geforce 4 graphics cards and the one that was blocked from running on anything with an Ati card in? This is that game! they have changed the front end a little to try to disguise it but underneath it all is a 10+ year old game that still plays as it did back then. I liked this game back when I was playing Team Fortress Classic and it's still fun now in between Battlefield. More improtantly though, you don't need an Nvidia card to run it, hell you barely need anything more than a calculator to run this! 
This game is a great distraction to play between serious gaming, and it will keep you scratching you head for ages. Don't pay full price though.
Fun and cheap, what's not to like.

I'm very glad that this game was greenlit. I'm not a specialist of such type games, but I remember that I spent nice time many years ago building bridges playing very old games. And this game gives me the same fun. Maybe it isn't the best game in it's genre and UI could be better but... IT GIVES ME SO MUCH FUN!
One big disadvantage for me is that I thought that I will be able to play it on my tablet using touchscreen, but it's not the best idea.

VERDICT:
If you're a fan of puzzle games and want to try something different, try this game, this may be it.
If you're a fan of bridge building games, you definitely should try this.

the logic of this game was annoying as hell, and the physics OH my god! get me a gun now! But i have finished it and got 100% so I'm must have enjoyed it.

Not what I expected it to be. Kind of a let down. More or less a direct port from the mobile app. The overly-cartoonish map and UI, paired with an extremely magnified resolution, make it difficult to enjoy this particular bridge construction game.

I'm just glad I got it in a bundle. It might be to others' tastes, but I would recommend caution before diving in. Maybe just buy the mobile app instead.
A real simple bridge building game. I like it.

Want to learn something about physics? Go ahead, but don't take it too serious. And please don't play it, when you are gonna have to build a bridge in real life. Especially the second world with all those funny bridges where your trucks literaly have to jump the gap.

I was too dump to solve all the bridge on my own, especially when it came to the trucks. But there are a lot of solutions on the net. Sometimes I had pretty much the same idea but put the junction slightly different and all collapses.

If you are looking for a casual game and don't want to play hours of hours this could be what you are looking for. But I would suggest grabbing it on sale or in a bundle.

02/12/2016
The science involved in bridge construction seems exciting in a conversation, but on paper it's a nightmare, which is something I'm certain engineering students swiftly realize three months into their second year of a university stint that their parents had funded through eight grueling years of overtime at Walmart so that their only child, thanks to the barren wasteland that is the mother's fertility, could have a better chance at life than they ever did.

You can thank me later.
money

Little physics game that had consistent physics. It was nice to see you re-run the simulation over and over again with little to no variation - meaning your solutions were not luck based. Overall it was a fun and challenging game as you are forced to self learn different tricks and understand how the physics engine calculates the stress.
Great little physics game 9/10!
mofugga bes gam
Very addicting

I'm quite satisfied with this game, to be honest... I didn't expect that this game was going to be so fun as it is, I loved how the physic works here, the developers made an excellent job with it. About my experience, I can say that the first levels were quite easy, even I thought that this game wasn't so hard after all, but when I reached the highest levels of the game, I realized that this is a great game, I can assure you that this game is gonna break your mind, excellent.

8.2/10 - One of My Favorite Bridge Builders

I've played several bridge builders and this is one of my favorites. The game has a good variety of puzzles, lots of locations on the map and several levels of safety vehicles to test on your bridge. (car, truck, semi)

Completed the first map which consists of about 40 bridge building puzzles that were challenging but achievable. There are other maps to play as well, so I’m looking forward to continuing on.
 
I personally wouldn't pay the full $10; but there are tons of levels and I'm having lots of fun solving these bridge building puzzles. Look for on sale or in bundles!

I don't agree with the price, although I practically got it for free thanks to humble bundle, It's a good hour or two of entertainment if you can get it for like $3
For what it is. Great time killer. 8) and can be very funny with friends helping you at your side lol
Not a great game.
It's a super cool game and u will have fun while playing it!
da best
10/10 would impulse buy again.

Bridge Constuctor itself is definetly the hardest game of the series.
In the first moment it looks like Bridge Constructor Playground but it's quite more than that. 2 different maps, 8 different islands. One map has normal bridges, one has crazy bridges. Crazy bridges mean you have to build bridges with ramps and your test vehicle will fly through the air. 

When you have completed all this, then comes the freaky part. You'll get a little more budget. But now there is this super heavy tank truck. Damnit, I hate it when it explodes and rips your bridge off into pieces. If you like some challenge you can try to do some of the bridges just wooden. But at least at the tank truck challenge you must use every idea you have to get it over ...
I only bought this because of Vinesauce. Other than that, this game is shit.

Very fun game that will make you smile and laugh :D

You build bridges and then watch when your trucks or other vehicles try get to the other side...and that is too hilarious sometimes :D

I played free version (which only has 8 levels) on my phone and definatly wanted to buy it for computer :)
Alright game on Android, but crashes under Linux. Disappointing.
sad that poly bridge took this game and bumped the gfx up and they get all the credit

This game has a lot of promise, but is both too frustrating and too annoying.

Frustrating: I know this is a puzzle game, and I like puzzle games, but the game's only hint is 'use triangles', which is massively unhelpful. There's poor feedback about where your bridge went wrong, and sometimes the bridges seem to break apart unexpectedly.

Annoying: If you want to exit, and goodness forbid you're building or testing a bridge when this happens, you have to go through 4-6 screens to exit properly. In addition, while you can turn off the annoying music, you can't turn off the annoying and quite loud sound.

In conclusion, Poly Bridge is still early access, but it feels much more polished than this.

Sick game.

It's a true challenge game if you don't watch any guides.

On sale for only $1.49, I really wanted to like this game. It actually should have been called Get Angry At Wood Triangles Simulator 2013. Despite the physics being horrendous and bridges frequently exploding upon construction, there are not nearly as many funny moments as I expected. Instead, the game just feels frustrating to get through. If you'll excuse me, I'm going to gorge myself on Fritos until the pain goes away.
Want bad physics? Want to fucking rage and want to kill you, yourself and the first one to say hi to you? Play this!
It seemed little too simple and cartoonish. I couldn't find which edges stess out more etc.

This game is NOT the game that I remember playing a long time ago. This is a toy version. The physics are terrible, the bridges are not realistic. There are no special effects owhen bridges collapse, just sorry graphics and lame physics.

Very fun and addictive game. Just be careful playing on a Mac, because your progress will vanish every time you upgrade OSX. I only play it on my PC now for that reason. But it's so fun, I just started over each time I lost everything.
This game makes me want to take a bath in pubic hair, and clean it of in lean while crying. 8/8
hi

Pretty good simulation puzzle game.  Feels a little light on content.  The only progression in the game is that the gap gets wider each level.  You only have four materials to choose from, and very limited funds, which is what makes it a puzzle game rather than a creative sandbox.  Fun but repetitive. Gameplay: https://youtu.be/cKH_z1RkpQk

It is fun and simple game, do not expect to much from it. Buy it in a sale and you will waste no money. It is good entertainment when you need to take your mind of other things. Go build a bridge ...
Only irritating thing for me is constant reminding to connect with facebook.

Yeah its fun for about 2-3 hours 
The lack of an freebuild mode or "creative" mode kinda shorten the game length.
Need to waste time? Not enough time to fap? Simply play Bridge Constructor! The auto-fap 3000! // 10/10 - IGN 'When faps are hard to fit into your schedule'.
The question is why do I have two bridge building simulation games in my steam library. This goes along with all other simulators: great if you're autistic.

This is a good puzzle game in the form of building bridges, The game starts out easy then proceeds to get more difficult as the levels go on. My only complaint is that you aren't building bridges for long terms use, just to get two vehicles past a canyon.

This game seems to be balanced so that reasonable-looking functional bridges are too expensive to build for any given scenario. This might be in pursuit of making it a better puzzle game, but I thought it just made it irritating. I'd rather have a difficult puzzle and the tools to solve it (Infinifactory, as an example) than a simple puzzle that I have to kludge something together for because my tools have been taken away. Not fun past the first few puzzles.
These idiots made an achievement that requires a Facebook account.

How come this game has been greenlit? I have played many bridge constructor games on Flash, standalone or mobile apps; not all of them were good but most featured better physics and playability/UI than this one.
The physics really sucks: it's slow like it was slow-motion, moreover it's impossible to speed it up! So one waits for way too long for the trucks to cross a bridge. Even my girlfriend who knows only about Flash and casual games, looking at the game and watching the trucks fall off a breaking bridge, said it looks amateurish.

Note to dev: scaling down the objects & world so that the physics simulation would run more realistically on Unity (which the game seems to be made off) is really no big deal!

Some GUI textures are even 'optimized' with a 256-colors palette, like if our computers lack bytes of VRAM...

Everything from the game system to the GUI feels mobile (which is an excellent thing... on small touchscreens), nothing seems to have been redesigned for computers... except the steep price!

I played up to the middle of the second world; I wanted to give this game a chance. But it's frustrating and repetitive, and the appealing settings/bridges shown in the promotional screenshots had yet to come into play.

This is definitely a bad game, boring and disrespectful to the players who are just treated as customers (what's this omnipresent 'Share on Facebook' on a single player premium computer game?!)
Avoid at all cost.


PS: This game is one more example of Greenlight picking crappy uninspired games (mostly from the genres/models the people already know) while disregarding many unique gems.

This is not a good building game.

First off, the controls. The controls are completely mouse driven, with the keyboard only being able to move the viewing area around the screen. The mouse also can move the viewing area, meaning moving too close to the edge makes the whole screen move, which just pisses me off to no end.
If you want to place multiple pieces on after another, after you place on, the next one is automatically connected, which, while may seem useful, is not. It mainly gets in the way and makes red show up way too often.

Second, the physics. The physics suck. Minecraft has better physics.
Also, the wooden structures and wires do hit the road. if the top of yout bridge falls over, it falls through the road.

Read other negative reviews for the physics. I cannot express my dislike well in words.
best fun you can have is building a failing bridge just to watch the crash , or you chould try finish the game but good luck with that one , ima stick to crashing trucks .
surprisingly enjoyable little game :)

The first bridge constructor game I've played and definitely wont be the last. I've completed the first 2 islands and half of the third. 

Challenging, but rewarding. Even funny in some contexts.

Audio - Enjoyable, but i muted it in favor of my own music
Visuals - Simple, even.

An okay bridge building game with various resources to build with. The biggest downside is that to make the game more challenging, they mostly limit you to JUST using wood, which is annoying. Definitely an iOS port but it still works as intended. Could've been a much better game.
Boring
Its very fun.

[h1]Simple But Solid[/h1]
The game runs very well and the consistency of the materials is great. This allows players to easily figure out what is going wrong with their design through trial and error without any element of randomness. However, the game limits creativity as the design from the tutorial, that is the triangles, sets the foundation for all of the bridges for the rest of the game. However that being said this makes it realistic and an easy going game to play. It is worth playing if you just want to relax with a little bit of a challenge.
:maaad:
complete fucking waste of money

Not worth the money that steam is asking for it. I paid £0.69 and used it to get through periods of time where I was unable to play anyting that took over 20 minutes at a time to play. It gave me moderate entertainment but was very simple and not a lot to the game. If you can get it for £0.69 the consider it other wise well..... its up to you really.

I like this game

the only thing that makes it not worth it is the price tag... it is $10 for a mobile phone port

it has facebook sharing built into it and it asks "do you want to share this on facebook" at the end of every level.....

but if you can get it on sale for like $3-$5 go for it

I think it needs to be said that the game does fail as a physics based puzzle game, in that I've had at least one moment where simply running the simulation a second time without changing anything achieved a different result than it did the first time I ran it (a part did not break at the homestretch of my bridge, netting me both the win for the level and the Pinocchio achievement).  I can only report this happening once because I instantly came here to point this out in a review, and before now I hadn't even tried such a boneheaded maneuver when things went wrong (I only did it this time to reevaluate what was wrong with the bridge!).  I'm having trouble recommending a physics based puzzle game where you can get different results by trying the same thing more than once (isn't that the definition of insanity?), so I will have to give this a thumbs down.

However, I would also point out to prospective buyers that the game actually is a lot of fun, so I'm hitting "no" on a bit of an edge case.  If the game seams appealing to you, you probably should still buy this, it just cheapens the experience a bit to realize that there may be an (indeterminable amount of an) element of pure luck involved here.

now i know why engineers try to use cheaper materials and why some times they go to jell..

great, not easy, game!

The bottom line for me is that I'm not sure that I learned anything about building bridges from this game. I don't expect to become an expert from playing a game, but I would prefer more learning and less randomly placing items and hoping it works. Also, I'm really glad I don't have to drive over any of the bridges I built.

i might have accidently killed some families with my crappy bridge building skills 
8/10

Bridge Constuctor itself is definetly the hardest game of the series.
In the first moment it looks like Bridge Constructor Playground but it's quite more than that.
2 different maps 8 different islands. One island has normal bridges one has crazy bridges.
That means you have to build bridges with ramps and your test vehicles will fly through the air. 

When you have completed all this then comes the freaky part. You'll get a little more budget but now there is this super heavy tank truck. Damnit I hate it when it explodes and rips your bridge off into pieces. If you like some challenge you can try to do some of the bridges just wooden. But at least at the tank truck challenge you must use every idea you have to get him over ...

TL;DR it's a dumbed down, oversimplified, easier, and crappier imitation of Bridge It.

The level design is extremely easy and extremely repetitive.  Only a few levels in the entire game, using the biggest truck (the tanker), actually took more than 1 try.   I found myself building almost exactly the same bridge a dozen times on a dozen slightly different maps.  Then I had to repeat each of those maps again with a bigger truck.   Bridge constructor has slightly more levels than bridge it but the levels are so repetitive that effectively Bridge It has 3 times more levels.

The building materials are ridiculously strong, to the point that you can bridge a big gap with nothing but road layed in an inverted arch.   Also, there's less variety in building materials.

When you mouseover beams it often highlights a different beam from the one that will actually be affected when you click.

It panders to you with stupid achievements and crowd cheering sounds that you get just for breathing.   This is typical of pretty much every corporate game since Wrath of the Lich King, but still, it's really stupid.

Yeah. It is fun at first.... then compulsion sets in and you NEED to make things work. I don't think the physics are too well done. My job is in construction. I like to think that after 10 years, I have a pretty good handle on construction physics. The arch means nothing to this game. The rest of the physics seem well done. You figure what the game wants to work, then mix and repeat, over and over and over. 

If you can grab this for $5, Go ahead! But...... I don't think that it is fully worth $10.

I was spoiled with World of Goo. Buy that instead. If you already have World of Goo..... don't expect that from this game.
I think it is just fun to fool around but after a while it gets too repetitive
This game is very addictive and lots of fun. 10/10 best bridge building game ever!

Bridge Constructor is a simple to jump into yet challenging physics bridge builder that challenges the player to solve puzzles.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_lMY8h2Dw2I

This game is too short and simplistic overall, and the controls are very clearly made for a tablet. Once you figure out the three major types of bridges, you can beat any challenge by building the correct type of bridge. Maybe buy it on sale, but it's not worth full price.
Crashes on linux after a few right click delete.
Really fun but for me medival was better, this game is much bigger than medival but it doesn't have as much character.
Good game.

I loved the game 'Bridge Builder' when I was younger and when I found 'Bridge Constructor' in the Steam Summer Sale I bought it (and the whole bundle of 'Bridge Constructor') at first sight. 
The graphics are nice and there are many challenging and also very funny levels (look at my screenshots). Furthermore there are good guides helping you, if you cannot find a solution for your level. I reached 100% of the achievements after 12 hours playing this game, because I wanted to try it by myself before using a guide.
My conclusion; I would absolutely recommend this game.
Boring Game
This is a mobile port. All that was needed to say has been said.
